questions.text: The volume of a cylindrical bar is to be determined. Its length and cross-sectional radius are found to have 1% and 3/2 % error respectively. The maximum percentage error in the calculated volume is:

A train runs at a velocity +15 m/s relative to earth. A passenger in the train throws a stone with a speed of 15 m/s in a direction opposite to the movement of train. The velocity of stone, relative to earth is:

A block of mass m moving with a velocity v, strikes horizontally, the free end of a spring of force constant k, so as to compress it, whose other end is fixed to a rigid support. If F be the maximum force offered by the spring on the block, then F is proportional to:

A cube of mass 'm' rests on a rough horizontal surface. The coefficient of friction (μ) is large enough to impede any kind of sliding, before it topples. The minimum value of horizontal force F required to topple it is:
A block rests on a rough inclined plane (with coefficient of friction μ) with 30° angle of inclination. If the frictional force is 20 N, then the mass of the block (in kg) is: (take g=10 m/s²)
A force F acts on a particle at rest at time t=0. If its kinetic energy varies as the square of time t, then F is proportional to:
A force F = 5√2(i+j) acts at a point on the origin. The magnitude of torque about point (1,0) due to F is:
A uniform wire having linear mass density λ is given the shape of a circular coil of radius R. Moment of inertia of this ring about any of its diameter is:
A 10 cm long capillary tube is dipped in water, wherein water rises to 4 cm height. If the entire set up is installed in a freely falling elevator, the length of water column in the tube is:
options: 10 cm without any overflow of water

questions.text: In an adiabatic process P ∝ T³. Then γ(=Cp/Cv) for the gas is:
Two identical coherent sound waves A and B reach a point in the same phase, producing a resultant sound wave C. The loudness of C is n dB higher than that of B. the value of n is about:
The rms speed of gas molecules is c, whose average number of degrees of freedom per molecule is 6. The speed of sound in the gas is:
A particle undergoes SHM with amplitude A along X-axis, represented by d²x/dt² + Bx = 0. Its maximum speed will be:

Two identical conducting spheres carrying different charges attract each other. They are then brought into contact and then taken to their original positions. They repel each other, with a force of same magnitude as that of the initial attractive force. The ratio between the initial charges on the spheres is:
Each of 27 identical drops of a conducting liquid is charged up to a potential of V0. The potential on the surface, if they coalesce to form a bigger spherical drop is:
A parallel plate capacitor having capacitance C has charges on different surfaces of its two plates as shown in figure. The potential difference between the capacitor plate is:
For a uniform electric field E = E0i, find the electric potential at X = x0, if that at x = 0 is zero:
A 10 metre long wire has a resistance of 12 per meter. It is bent to a circular shape. If A and B are two points on the ring, between which the resistance is 2.4Ω, then the length of minor arc AB is:
A long solenoid has 20 turns per centimeter winding. The current necessary to produce a magnetic field of 20 mT on its axis inside it is:

A galvanometer with coil resistance of 50Ω, yields full scale deflection corresponding to a current of 10 mA. The resistance to be connected in a manner, with the coil, to convert, it into an ammeter to measure current upto 1.0A is:
The correct relation for magnetic permeability of a medium is: (symbols have usual meaning)
If Tc = Curie temperature for a ferro-magnetic substance, then correct dependence of its magnetic susceptibility X on temperature T, above Tc is:
A glass convex lens is dipped completely in a liquid of refractive index μ=1.5. Then its focal length will:
Light is incident on a transparent plate at an angle of incidence 60°. If reflected and refracted rays are mutually perpendicular, then refractive index of the substance of plate is:
A convex lens is in contact kept coaxially with a concave lens. The ratio of their respective powers is 2/3 and focal length of the combination is 30 cm. Their individual focal lengths are:
A light ray passes through an equilateral prism placed in the position of minimum angle of deviation. If the angle of emergence is 3/4th of the prism angle, then the angle of deviation is:
A 10W sodium vapour lamp converts 90% of the energy consumed into light. If sodium light has a wavelength 590 nm and h=6.63×10⁻³⁴ J·s, find the rate of photon emission by the lamp per second:

A certain photometal has a threshold frequency of f0. Electrons are ejected with a maximum velocity of 4×10⁶ m/s when light of frequency 2f0 is incident on it. The corresponding velocity for incident light of 5f0 frequency is: (in m/s)
A parallel 60 W power beam incident normally on a plane surface gets 40% absorbed and remaining reflected by the latter. The force exerted by the beam on the surface is:
The de-Broglie wavelength of a particle of kinetic energy K is λ, the wavelength of the particle, if its kinetic energy becomes K/4 is
In an LR circuit current at t=0 is 20 A. After 2s it reduces to 18 A. The time constant of the circuit is: (in second)
Figure shows a square loop of side 0.5 m and resistance 10Ω. The magnetic field has a magnitude B=1.0 T. The work done in pulling the loop out of the field slowly and uniformly in 2.0 s is:

A plastic hemisphere has a radius of curvature of 8 cm and an index of refraction of 1.6. On the axis halfway between the plane surface and the spherical one (4 cm from each) is a small object O. The distance between the two images when viewed along the axis from the two sides of the hemisphere is approximately:
Magnetic moment due to the motion of the electron in nth energy state of hydrogen atom is proportional to:
The equation represents ²₁H + ²₁H → ⁴₂He²⁺ + 2e⁻ + 26MeV

The figure shows the variation of photocurrent with anode potential for a photosensitive surface for three different radiations. Let Ia, Ib and Ic be the intensities and fa, fb, and fc be the frequencies for the curves a, b and c respectively:
A projectile is launched at an angle α with the horizontal with a velocity 20 m/s. After 10 s, its inclination with horizontal is β. The value of tan β will be: (g=10 m/s²).

A body of mass is taken from earth surface to the height h equal to twice the radius of earth (Re), the increase in potential energy will be: (g = acceleration due to gravity on the surface of earth).

A satellite of 10³ kg mass is revolving in circular orbit of radius 2R. If 10⁴R/6 J energy is supplied to the satellite, it would revolve in a new circular orbit of radius (use g=10 m/s², R= radius of earth).
Three charged concentric nonconducting shells are given as shown in figure. The electrostatic potential at point A is:
A parallel plate capacitor with square plates is filled with four dielectrics of dielectric constants K1, K2, K3, K4 arranged as shown in the figure. The effective dielectric constant K will be:

A capacitor, C1 = 6μF is charged to a potential difference of V0 = 5V using a 5 V battery. The battery is removed and another capacitor, C2 = 12μF is inserted in place of the battery. When the switch S' is closed, the charge flows between the capacitors for some time until equilibrium condition is reached. What are the charges q1 and q2 on the capacitors C1 and C2 when equilibrium condition is reached?
A resistance is shown in the figure. Its value and tolerance are given respectively by: (Red, Violet, Orange, Silver)

A galvanometer of resistance 100Ω when connected in series with 400Ω measures a voltage of up to 10 V. The value of resistance required to convert the galvanometer into ammeter to read up to 10A is x × 10⁻² Ω. The value of x is:

An electron gun is placed inside a long solenoid of radius R on its axis. The solenoid has n turns/length and carries a current I. The electron gun shoots an electron along the radius of the solenoid with speed v. If the electron does not hit the surface of the solenoid, maximum possible value of v is:
Two conducting circular loops A and B are placed in the same plane with their centers coinciding. The mutual inductance between them is: (assuming b >> a)
An ideal resistance R, ideal inductance L, ideal capacitance C and AC voltmeters V1, V2, V3 and V4 are connected to an AC source as shown. At resonance,
options: reading in V3 = reading in V1
reading in V1 = reading in V2
reading in V2 = reading in V4
reading in V2 = reading in V3

questions.text: An infinitely long wire has uniform linear charge density λ = 2 nC/m. The net flux through a Gaussian cube of side length √3 cm, if the wire passes through any two corners of the cube that are maximally displaced from each other, would be x N m² C⁻¹ where x is: [Neglect any edge effects and use 1/4πε₀ = 9×10⁹ SI units]

Two point charges 4q and -q are fixed on the x-axis at x = -d/2 and x = d/2, respectively. If the third point charge 'q' is taken from the origin to x = d along the semicircle as shown in the figure, the energy of the charge will:

Two identical electric point dipoles have dipole moments p1 = pi and p2 = -pi and are held on the x-axis at distance 'a' from each other. When released, they move along the x-axis with the direction of their dipole moments remaining unchanged. If the mass of each dipole is 'm', their speed when they are infinitely far apart is:

A wire of length 2L is made by joining two wires A and B of same length but different radii r and 2r and made of the same material. It is vibrating at a frequency such that the joint of the two wires forms a node. If the number of antinodes in wire A is p and that in B is q, then ratio p:q is:

A string is clamped at both ends and it is vibrating in its 4th harmonic. The equation of the stationary wave is y = 0.3 sin(0.157x) cos(200πt). The length of the string is: (All quantities are in SI units.)
The length of metallic wire is l1 when tension in it is T1. It is l2 when the tension is T2. The original length of the wire will be:
Two protons are kept at a separation of 10 nm. Let Fn and Fe be the nuclear force and the electromagnetic force between them:
options: Fn and Fe differ only slightly

questions.text: A solid cylinder of radius R made of a material of thermal conductivity K1 is surrounded by a cylindrical shell of inner radius R and outer radius 2R made of a material of thermal conductivity K2. The two ends of the combined system are maintained at two different temperatures. Then there is no loss of heat across the cylindrical surface and the system is in steady state. The effective thermal conductivity of the system is
1 gram of ice is mixed with 1 gram of steam. Assuming no loss of energy, at thermal equilibrium, the temperature of the mixture is

Mass numbers of the elements A, B, C and D are 30, 60, 90 and 120 respectively. Their binding energy per nucleon are 5 MeV, 8.5 MeV, 8 MeV and 7 MeV respectively. Then, in which of the following reactions energy is released? (1) D → 2B (2) C → B + A (3) B → 2A
If ε₀ and μ₀ are the permittivity and permeability of free space and ε and μ are the corresponding quantities for a medium, then refractive index of the medium is
In the figure shown, if the diode forward voltage drop is 0.2 V, the voltage difference between A and B is
The reverse break down voltage of a Zener diode is 5.6 V in the given circuit. The current Iz through the Zener diode is:
